why_it_matters: Hunger-related wooziness supports the nausea/hypoglycemia picture and reinforces small frequent meals counseling, but it also partly conflicts with the documented denial of dizziness and should be captured accurately.
Discontinuation of contraception and confirmation of no regular medications plus only occasional OTC use is core content of the documented medication review and affects prenatal safety guidance.
A vasovagal fainting history with blood draws is an injury risk during this visit's extensive phlebotomy and requires the documented accommodation to prevent syncope-related harm.
Activity, work, and diet counseling documents anticipatory guidance appropriate to an initial prenatal visit but is low clinical risk if omitted.
Announcement timing is a non-medical social discussion that has no bearing on clinical management.
